figures.caption: Table 1: Molecular band systems of the present radiation model.
Figure 1: Flowchart of the coupling framework used in this work.
Figure 2: Geometry of the Plasmatron X torch [57].
Figure 4: EM solver mesh. The mesh domain highlighted in green overlaps with the plasma solver mesh. Coil locations are marked in red.

Figure 5: Plasma field inside the Plasmatron X torch. (a) Top: heavy-species temperature Th, bottom: electro-vibrational temperature Tve, and (b) top: streamlines, bottom: axial velocity. (pa = 5kPa, P = 200 kW, η = 50%).
Figure 6: Distribution of the electromagnetic quantities inside the torch. (a) Joule heating, (b) Lorentz forces (top: radial component, bottom: axial component). (pa = 5kPa, P = 200 kW, η = 50%).
Figure 7: Radial temperature profiles obtained using the NLTE and LTE simulations. (a) x = 0.15m (mid-torch location), and (b) torch outlet. (pa = 5kPa, P = 200 kW, η = 50%).
Figure 8: LTE and NLTE radial profiles of Joule heating and Lorentz force at x = 0.15m (coil region). (pa = 5kPa, P = 200 kW, η = 50%).

Figure 9: Radiative heat loss and Joule heating distribution normalized by corresponding peak values for nitrogen plasma: (a) 1 kPa, (b) 5 kPa, (c) 10 kPa and (d) 30 kPa, all at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%). Top: ΩR, bottom: ΩJ.

Figure 10: Radiative heat loss and Joule heating distribution normalized by corresponding peak values for air plasma: (a) 1 kPa, (b) 5 kPa, (c) 10 kPa and (d) 30 kPa, all at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%). Top: ΩR, bottom: ΩJ.

Figure 11: Distribution of electron mole-fraction Xe and electro-vibrational temperature Tve in the torch for air plasma. (a) 1 kPa case, and (b) 30 kPa case, both at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%).
Figure 12: Total radiative heat loss in the ICP torch as a percent of input power at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%).
Figure 13: Electron number density distribution at 30 kPa, and 200 kW (with η = 50%). Top: nitrogen plasma, bottom: air plasma.
Figure 14: Radial profiles of heavy-species temperature for nitrogen plasma at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%): (a) x = 0.15m (mid-torch location) and (b) x = 0.36m (nozzle exit).

Figure 15: Radial profiles of electro-vibrational temperature for nitrogen plasma at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%) : (a) x = 0.15m (mid-torch location) and (b) x = 0.36m (nozzle exit).
Figure 16: Radial profiles of heavy-species temperature for air plasma at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%) : (a) x = 0.15m (mid-torch location) and (b) x = 0.36m (nozzle exit).
Figure 17: Radial profiles of electro-vibrational temperature for air plasma at a fixed operating power of 200 kW (with η = 50%) : (a) x = 0.15m (mid-torch location) and (b) x = 0.36m (nozzle exit).

Figure 18: Total radiative heat loss in the ICP torch as a percent of input power : (a) nitrogen, and (b) air plasmas. The black dot implies the actual data point for which the simulation is carried out.
Figure 19: Decomposition of the total radiative heat loss in the ICP torch as a percent of input power into the UV, Visible, and IR wavelength ranges. (pa = 101.3 kPa, P = 350 kW, η = 50%).
Figure 20: Radiative heat loss distribution in the torch for air plasma. Top: without selfabsorption (optically thin assumption), bottom: with self-absorption. (pa = 101.3 kPa, P = 350 kW, η = 50%).
